text:
Assistant: Now I have a clear picture. Here's what tenex-edge actually reads from `~/.tenex-edge/llms.json` right now (per `src/llmconfig.rs`, the only consumer, called from `src/distill.rs`):

**Actually used:**
- The role key `"edge-distillation"` (the *only* role name the code looks up — via `resolve_role("edge-distillation")`)
- `configurations[<name>].provider` and `configurations[<name>].model` — just those two fields, nothing else on the object [...] - `configurations[<name>].provider` and `configurations[<name>].model` — just those two fields, nothing else on the object

**Not read by anything in the codebase (dead config):**
- `"default"` at the top level
- `"summarization"`, `"supervision"`, `"promptCompilation"`, `"categorization"`, `"contextDiscovery"`, `"firewall"` — none of these role names are looked up anywhere
- Inside configuration entries: `"variants"`, `"description"` (nested under variants), `"backend"`, `"command"`, `"args"`, `"permissionPolicy"` — `resolve_role_in` only ever calls `.get("provider")` and `.get("model")` on a configuration object, so these are silently ignored [...] - The `"auto claude"` entry's `provider: "meta"` and the two `"acp"`-provider entries (`"claude code acp"`, `"codex acp"`) resolve to `None` even if referenced by a role, since only `openrouter`, `ollama`, and `claude-cli` are supported providers (see the comment in llmconfig.rs) [...] - `"summarization"`, `"supervision"`, `"promptCompilation"`, `"categorization"`, `"contextDiscovery"`, `"firewall"` — none of these role names are looked up anywhere [...] - `"default"` at the top level
